rationale: The cited paper is used for more than a passing mention: the citing article references it as prior empirical evidence of reference-price effects in packaged-goods transaction data and again when discussing how reference-price terms can be specified in the model. This directly informs the citing paper's conceptual setup and empirical specification choices. While the citing paper does not adopt the cited paper's full heterogeneity framework, it relies on it as part of the core reference-price literature it builds from in moving to a B2B setting.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Chang, Mayhew, and Sun (2001) as part of the conceptual and empirical base for internal reference-price effects and for reference-price variable specification. It then develops a new B2B simultaneous price-and-quantity model on top of that prior stream.
